description: Cached AI-facing semantics for common Niagara modules. This catalog complements live schema/topology/data exports; it is not a substitute for listing actual module inputs after adding a module.

entries.name: InitializeParticle
entries.summary: Initializes common particle attributes during Particle Spawn: lifetime, color, size, mass, sprite/mesh defaults, and initial attribute values.

inputs.name: Lifetime
inputs.role: Initial particle lifetime range or value.

inputs.name: Sprite Size
inputs.role: Initial sprite dimensions when sprite rendering is used.

inputs.name: Mesh Scale
inputs.role: Initial mesh particle scale when mesh rendering is used.

outputs.name: initialized particle attributes
outputs.role: Provides the base values that later update modules modify.

side_effects: Defines the baseline visual identity of each particle at spawn.

key_inputs.name: Uniform Sprite Size
key_inputs.meaning: Initial uniform sprite size when that mode is active.

critical_inputs.name: Lifetime
critical_inputs.why: Too short can make successful spawn edits appear invisible.

critical_inputs.name: Color
critical_inputs.why: Later color modules may multiply this instead of replacing it.

critical_inputs.name: Sprite Size|Mesh Scale
critical_inputs.why: Renderer type decides which size input is visible.

stack_requirements: Place in particle_spawn before update-time color/size/physics modules.
required_followups: After color or scale edits, inspect later modules that write or multiply Particles.Color, Particles.SpriteSize, or Particles.MeshScale.
pitfalls: If a later color/scale module overwrites or multiplies the same attribute, changing InitializeParticle alone may appear to have no effect.
entries.notes: Usually one of the first Particle Spawn modules. Re-export and inspect actual inputs because module versions expose size/color modes differently.

entries.name: AddVelocity
entries.summary: Adds initial particle velocity during spawn, usually through directional, cone, or random distributions.

inputs.name: Cone Axis
inputs.role: Main direction for cone velocity. 0,0,1 means upward.

inputs.name: Cone Angle
inputs.role: Angular spread in degrees; 0 is a tight stream.

outputs.name: velocity delta
outputs.role: Contributes to initial Particles.Velocity.

side_effects: Changes initial motion direction and speed before update solvers run.

critical_inputs.name: Velocity Speed
critical_inputs.why: Magnitude can be zero even when direction inputs look correct.

critical_inputs.name: Cone Axis
critical_inputs.why: Controls direction for cone/distribution modes.

critical_inputs.name: Cone Angle
critical_inputs.why: Large spread can hide intended direction.

stack_requirements: Usually particle_spawn; needs a later motion solver/update path to keep velocity moving position.
required_followups: Verify Particle Update contains SolveForcesAndVelocity or another position integration path.
pitfalls: Needs a solver such as SolveForcesAndVelocity in Particle Update to keep velocity affecting position over time.
entries.notes: Often paired with ShapeLocation and SolveForcesAndVelocity.

entries.name: ShapeLocation
entries.summary: Places particles on or inside a procedural shape during spawn.

inputs.name: Shape
inputs.role: Shape mode such as sphere, box, cylinder, torus, plane, or ring depending on version.

outputs.name: spawn position
outputs.role: Initial particle position distribution.

side_effects: Controls spatial distribution of newly spawned particles.

key_inputs.name: Shape Origin
key_inputs.meaning: Origin of the shape used for spawning.

key_inputs.name: Sphere Radius
key_inputs.meaning: Radius when sphere-like shape modes are active.

key_inputs.name: Uniform Count
key_inputs.meaning: Count used by uniform/discrete distribution modes.

critical_inputs.name: Shape
critical_inputs.why: This mode selects which radius/extent/count inputs are meaningful.

critical_inputs.name: Shape Origin
critical_inputs.why: Offsets all generated positions.

critical_inputs.name: Uniform Count
critical_inputs.why: Only relevant for discrete/uniform modes; not arbitrary point placement.

critical_static_switches.name: Shape
critical_static_switches.rule: List module inputs and confirm the active shape mode before setting shape-specific fields.

stack_requirements: Particle spawn location module; put before modules that offset or overwrite Particles.Position.
required_followups: After changing shape mode, list inputs again because shape-specific fields may appear/disappear.
pitfalls: For strict arbitrary point lists, ShapeLocation alone may not be enough; use a custom/local module or array input.
entries.notes: List actual inputs after adding because v1/v2 shape modules expose different names.

entries.name: SpawnRate
entries.summary: Continuously spawns particles at a rate over emitter time.

outputs.name: spawn count request
outputs.role: Contributes continuous spawn requests to the emitter.

side_effects: Changes particle population over time.

critical_inputs.name: Spawn Rate
critical_inputs.why: Main density control for continuous emission.

stack_requirements: Emitter update module; use burst instead for exact particle counts.
required_followups: If the user asks for exactly N particles, prefer SpawnBurst_Instantaneous or disable SpawnRate.
pitfalls: Use SpawnBurst_Instantaneous instead when the request is for exactly N particles.
entries.notes: Usually lives in Emitter Update, not Particle Spawn.

entries.name: SpawnBurst_Instantaneous
entries.summary: Spawns a fixed count of particles at a specified emitter time.

inputs.name: Spawn Count
inputs.role: Number of particles emitted by the burst.

inputs.name: Spawn Time
inputs.role: Emitter age at which the burst fires; 0 means immediately.

inputs.name: Loop Count Limit
inputs.role: How many emitter loops can emit this burst.

outputs.name: spawn count request
outputs.role: Adds a one-shot spawn request.

side_effects: Creates fixed particle populations or one-shot bursts.

key_inputs.name: Spawn Count
key_inputs.meaning: Number of particles emitted by the burst.

key_inputs.name: Spawn Time
key_inputs.meaning: Emitter age at which the burst fires.

key_inputs.name: Loop Count Limit
key_inputs.meaning: How many emitter loops can emit this burst.

critical_inputs.name: Spawn Count
critical_inputs.why: Exact particle count for one burst.

critical_inputs.name: Spawn Time
critical_inputs.why: A nonzero time delays the burst.

critical_inputs.name: Loop Count Limit
critical_inputs.why: Can repeat bursts across emitter loops if not limited.

stack_requirements: Emitter update module; often paired with long particle lifetime for persistent points.
required_followups: Check InitializeParticle lifetime and emitter loop behavior when exact visible count matters.
pitfalls: Particle lifetime must be long enough if the requested particles should remain visible.
entries.notes: Prefer this over SpawnRate when count must be exact.

entries.name: ScaleColor
entries.summary: Multiplies particle color and/or alpha during update.

inputs.name: Color Value To Scale
inputs.role: RGBA multiplier applied to particle color.

inputs.name: Scale RGB
inputs.role: RGB multiplier when RGB scale mode is active.

outputs.name: scaled color
outputs.role: Final color after multiplication.

side_effects: Tints or fades particles, often over lifetime.

key_inputs.name: Color Value To Scale
key_inputs.meaning: Color value multiplied into the particle color.

key_inputs.name: Scale RGB
key_inputs.meaning: RGB multiplier when Scale RGB mode is active.

critical_inputs.name: Color Value To Scale
critical_inputs.why: Multiplies existing color; not an absolute assignment.

critical_inputs.name: Scale Alpha
critical_inputs.why: Can make particles invisible even if RGB is correct.

critical_static_switches.name: Scale RGB|Scale Alpha mode
critical_static_switches.rule: Inspect actual inputs because some versions expose separate RGB/alpha switches or curves.

stack_requirements: Particle update color module; later color modules can override or multiply again.
required_followups: For absolute color changes, inspect InitializeParticle and any later Particles.Color writers.
pitfalls: Because it multiplies existing color, black input channels stay black. For absolute color, set InitializeParticle color or use a Set Parameters/local module.
entries.notes: If InitializeParticle already writes color, ScaleColor can further multiply it.

entries.name: GravityForce
entries.summary: Adds constant acceleration, usually downward gravity, during particle update.

outputs.name: force/acceleration
outputs.role: Contributes to accumulated force before solving.

side_effects: Changes velocity over time after force solving.

key_inputs.name: Gravity
key_inputs.meaning: Acceleration vector; negative Z is downward in typical world coordinates.

critical_inputs.name: Gravity
critical_inputs.why: Direction and magnitude of acceleration.

stack_requirements: Particle update force module; must execute before SolveForcesAndVelocity or equivalent solver.
required_followups: Verify a solver exists after this module and compile_status has no errors.
pitfalls: Requires SolveForcesAndVelocity after it. If no solver exists, force modules may appear to do nothing.
entries.notes: Good simple force for falling sparks or rising bubbles.

entries.name: CurlNoiseForce
entries.summary: Adds turbulent curl-noise force during particle update.

inputs.name: Noise Frequency
inputs.role: Spatial scale/frequency of the noise field.

outputs.name: turbulent force
outputs.role: Contributes noise-driven force before solving.

side_effects: Adds organic swirling/drifting motion.

key_inputs.name: Noise Frequency
key_inputs.meaning: Spatial scale of the noise field.

critical_inputs.name: Noise Strength
critical_inputs.why: Main force magnitude; zero makes the module effectively invisible.

critical_inputs.name: Noise Frequency
critical_inputs.why: Controls visual scale of turbulence.

stack_requirements: Particle update force module; must execute before SolveForcesAndVelocity or equivalent solver.
required_followups: Pair with Drag if motion becomes too chaotic, and verify solver order.
pitfalls: Needs SolveForcesAndVelocity later in Particle Update.
entries.notes: Use lower frequency for broad motion and higher frequency for choppy detail.

entries.name: Drag
entries.summary: Damps particle velocity over time, making motion slow down.

outputs.name: drag force
outputs.role: Opposes current velocity.

side_effects: Reduces speed and makes motion settle.

critical_inputs.name: Drag
critical_inputs.why: High values can suppress all other velocity/force edits.

stack_requirements: Particle update force/velocity damping module; place before/near solver according to stack defaults.
required_followups: If forces appear ineffective, check whether Drag is too high.
pitfalls: Excessive drag can hide velocity/force changes from other modules.
entries.notes: Useful with gravity, curl noise, or attraction to avoid runaway motion.

entries.name: PointAttractionForce
entries.summary: Attracts particles toward a target point.

inputs.name: Attractor Position
inputs.role: Target point used by the attraction force.

inputs.name: Attraction Radius
inputs.role: Distance over which attraction is applied.

outputs.name: attraction force
outputs.role: Pulls velocity/position toward the target through the solver.

side_effects: Creates gathering, orbit-like, or central pull behavior.

key_inputs.name: Attractor Position
key_inputs.meaning: Target point used by the attraction force.

key_inputs.name: Attraction Radius
key_inputs.meaning: Radius over which attraction is applied.

critical_inputs.name: Attractor Position
critical_inputs.why: Target point for the force.

critical_inputs.name: Attraction Strength
critical_inputs.why: Main magnitude; negative or tiny values change behavior dramatically.

critical_inputs.name: Attraction Radius
critical_inputs.why: Particles outside radius may not respond.

stack_requirements: Particle update force module; requires SolveForcesAndVelocity or equivalent integration after it.
required_followups: For particle-to-particle attraction, use a local module/simulation stage instead of this point force.
pitfalls: This is not pairwise particle-to-particle gravity. For inter-particle gravity, use a local module or simulation stage.
entries.notes: Often paired with Drag to prevent overshooting.

entries.name: VortexForce
entries.summary: Applies swirling/tangential force around an axis or point.

outputs.name: swirl force
outputs.role: Adds tangential force around a center/axis.

side_effects: Creates spiral, tornado, orbital, or whirlpool motion.

critical_inputs.name: Vortex Axis
critical_inputs.why: Defines the swirl axis.

critical_inputs.name: Vortex Center
critical_inputs.why: Defines the center of rotation.

critical_inputs.name: Vortex Strength
critical_inputs.why: Main tangential force magnitude.

stack_requirements: Particle update force module; requires SolveForcesAndVelocity or equivalent integration after it.
required_followups: Use Drag to stabilize if particles fly away, and verify solver order.
pitfalls: Needs SolveForcesAndVelocity; combine with Drag for stable vortex motion.
entries.notes: Search/list actual inputs because V2 names may differ by engine version.

entries.name: SolveForcesAndVelocity
entries.summary: Integrates accumulated forces and velocity to update particle position.

outputs.name: integrated motion
outputs.role: Applies force/velocity accumulation to particle movement.

side_effects: Makes force modules visibly affect particle position.
stack_requirements: Particle update solver; place after force/velocity modules that should affect position.
required_followups: If force modules do nothing, add or move this after them, then compile_status.
pitfalls: Force modules before this may do nothing visually if this solver is absent or disabled.
entries.notes: Place after force modules such as CurlNoiseForce, GravityForce, VortexForce, or PointAttractionForce.

entries.name: StaticMeshLocation
entries.summary: Spawns particles from a static mesh source, commonly surface/vertex/triangle sampling.

outputs.name: sampled mesh position
outputs.role: Initial particle location sampled from a static mesh.

side_effects: Makes particles originate from mesh geometry.

key_inputs.name: Static Mesh
key_inputs.meaning: The static mesh data interface used as the spawn source.

key_inputs.name: Sampling Mode
key_inputs.meaning: Controls how the static mesh is sampled.

critical_inputs.name: Static Mesh
critical_inputs.why: The actual mesh source; without a valid asset sampling produces no useful positions.

critical_inputs.name: Sampling Mode|Mesh Sampling Type
critical_inputs.why: Chooses surface/triangle/vertex behavior depending on module version.

critical_static_switches.name: Sampling Mode|Mesh Sampling Type
critical_static_switches.rule: For surface emission, explicitly choose a surface/triangle/vertex value after list_module_inputs.

stack_requirements: Particle spawn location module; place before modules that overwrite Particles.Position.
required_followups: Always list inputs after adding because static mesh module versions expose different sampling names.

common_edits.goal: spawn particles on a static mesh surface

pitfalls: Data-interface input names vary by module version. Always list inputs after adding.
entries.notes: Good for mesh surface emission.

entries.name: SkeletalMeshLocation
entries.summary: Spawns particles from skeletal mesh locations such as surface, vertices, or bones.

inputs.name: Mesh Sampling Type
inputs.role: Critical hidden/static switch. Selects whether the module samples mesh surface data or skeleton bones/sockets.

inputs.name: Location Mode
inputs.role: Controls how sampled mesh data becomes particle position.

outputs.name: sampled skeletal mesh position
outputs.role: Initial particle location from animated mesh data.

side_effects: Binds spawn distribution to a skeletal mesh source.

key_inputs.name: Skeletal Mesh
key_inputs.meaning: The skeletal mesh data interface used as the spawn source.

key_inputs.name: Mesh Sampling Type
key_inputs.meaning: Controls surface/triangle/vertex vs bone/socket sampling. Must be inspected with list_module_inputs because it can be hidden.

critical_inputs.name: Skeletal Mesh
critical_inputs.why: The data interface asset/user parameter; without it sampling may use defaults or fail.

critical_inputs.name: Mesh Sampling Type
critical_inputs.why: Critical hidden/static switch that decides mesh surface vs skeleton sampling.

critical_inputs.name: Location Mode
critical_inputs.why: Controls how sampled data is applied to Particles.Position.

critical_static_switches.name: Mesh Sampling Type
critical_static_switches.rule: For mesh surface emission, explicitly choose a surface/triangle/vertex value; do not leave this on a bone/socket/skeleton value.

stack_requirements: Particle spawn location module; list inputs immediately after adding because important switches may be hidden.
required_followups: After setting mesh/data-interface/user parameter, set Mesh Sampling Type explicitly and run compile_status.
pitfalls: Do not add obsolete modules named like Skeletal Mesh Surface Location. Search this module, add it, re-export, then list actual inputs.
Surface emission is not guaranteed by adding this module; the hidden/static switch Mesh Sampling Type may still point at bones or sockets.
entries.notes: Modern UE versions consolidate skeletal mesh sampling into this module plus input modes.

entries.name: SampleSkeletalMesh
entries.summary: Samples skeletal mesh data during update without necessarily spawning from it.

inputs.name: Sampling Coordinates
inputs.role: Coordinates or mode used to pick mesh data.

outputs.name: sampled position
outputs.role: Read mesh position data.

outputs.name: sampled normal
outputs.role: Read mesh normal/tangent data.

writes: Particles.* when assigned by exposed options
side_effects: Provides mesh-derived data that can drive position, color, velocity, or custom attributes.

critical_inputs.name: Skeletal Mesh
critical_inputs.why: The data source for all mesh samples.

critical_inputs.name: Mesh Sampling Type|Sampling Coordinates
critical_inputs.why: Controls whether reads use surface, vertex, triangle, bone, or coordinate inputs depending on version.

critical_static_switches.name: Mesh Sampling Type|Sampling Coordinates
critical_static_switches.rule: Inspect actual hidden/static-switch inputs before assuming surface sampling.

stack_requirements: Often particle_update or simulation_stage; does not automatically write Particles.Position unless wired/assigned.
required_followups: After adding, list inputs and inspect outputs/writes. Add assignment/local module if sampled data must drive position/color/velocity.
pitfalls: Sampling outputs may not write Particles.Position automatically. Use a local module or assignment if the requested attribute is not exposed.
entries.notes: Useful for follow-mesh or mesh-driven update effects.

entries.name: SampleParticlesFromOtherEmitter
entries.summary: Reads attributes from particles in another emitter through an Attribute Reader data interface.

inputs.name: Particle Index
inputs.role: Index or ID used to sample source particle data.

outputs.name: sampled attribute
outputs.role: Data read from another emitter.

side_effects: Creates inter-emitter dependency and enables constraints/links/chain effects.

critical_inputs.name: Emitter Name
critical_inputs.why: Must exactly match the source emitter identifier expected by the attribute reader.

critical_inputs.name: Particle Index|Particle ID
critical_inputs.why: Determines which source particle is sampled.

critical_inputs.name: Attribute
critical_inputs.why: Controls which value is read from the source emitter.

stack_requirements: Requires a valid source emitter and compatible sim target/data visibility.
required_followups: Verify emitter names, sim target, and source attribute existence after export.
pitfalls: Requires valid source emitter and stable indexing/ID strategy. GPU/CPU sim target matters.
entries.notes: Often appears in chain/constraint systems.

entries.name: GenerateDeathEvent
entries.summary: Emits an event when particles die.

outputs.name: death event
outputs.role: Event payload consumed by event handlers/receiver modules.

side_effects: Can trigger secondary emitters or event-driven logic on particle death.

critical_inputs.name: Event Name
critical_inputs.why: Must match the receiver/event handler configuration.

stack_requirements: Particle update event generator; requires event handler/receiver to consume the event.
required_followups: Check receiver event name and event handler source after adding.
pitfalls: Event handlers must be configured to receive the generated event.
entries.notes: Use when one particle system should spawn or notify another on death.

entries.name: ReceiveLocationEvent
entries.summary: Consumes location event payloads and usually spawns/positions particles from event data.

inputs.name: Execution Mode
inputs.role: How received events drive spawning or updates.

outputs.name: event payload data
outputs.role: Location/velocity/custom data from event source.

side_effects: Links one emitter's generated events to another emitter's particles.

critical_inputs.name: Event Name
critical_inputs.why: Must match the generator event name.

critical_inputs.name: Execution Mode
critical_inputs.why: Controls whether event payload drives spawn/update behavior.

critical_static_switches.name: Execution Mode
critical_static_switches.rule: Inspect actual input enum/static switch before assuming it spawns particles.

stack_requirements: Requires event handler wiring and a matching generator module.
required_followups: Verify event handler source_event and generator Event Name agree.
pitfalls: Needs matching generator event name and event handler wiring.
entries.notes: Useful for trails, secondary bursts, and event-driven spawn.

entries.name: CollisionQuery
entries.summary: Queries scene collision information such as depth/distance fields/ray hits depending on module configuration.

inputs.name: Trace Direction
inputs.role: Direction for collision/ray queries when exposed.

outputs.name: collision hit data
outputs.role: Surface position, normal, distance, or hit state.

side_effects: Enables collision response modules or custom hit logic.

critical_inputs.name: Trace Direction
critical_inputs.why: Direction for ray/depth-style queries when active.

critical_inputs.name: Query Distance
critical_inputs.why: Too small returns no hits.

critical_static_switches.name: Collision Query Mode|Trace Mode
critical_static_switches.rule: Collision modules often branch by query backend; list inputs before changing behavior.

stack_requirements: Particle update or simulation stage; behavior depends on CPU/GPU sim target and enabled collision data source.
required_followups: Verify sim target and collision data source/project settings if queries return nothing.
pitfalls: Collision behavior depends heavily on sim target and renderer/project collision settings.
entries.notes: Use with collision response modules for bouncing/sliding/stopping.

entries.name: CalculateLinkConstraint
entries.summary: Computes constraint forces or corrections between linked particles/points.

inputs.name: Stiffness
inputs.role: How strongly the constraint corrects error.

outputs.name: constraint correction
outputs.role: Position/velocity correction for maintaining link distance.

side_effects: Creates chain, rope, cloth-like, or linked-particle behavior.

critical_inputs.name: Rest Length
critical_inputs.why: Target distance for the constraint.

critical_inputs.name: Stiffness
critical_inputs.why: Correction strength; too high can explode without enough iterations/damping.

critical_inputs.name: Linked Particle
critical_inputs.why: Determines which neighbor/particle is constrained.

stack_requirements: Usually simulation_stage or carefully ordered particle_update; needs stable particle IDs/indices.
required_followups: Check ID/index setup and iteration/stage order before tuning stiffness.
pitfalls: Usually needs stable particle IDs/indices and may require simulation stages for iterative solving.
entries.notes: Useful reference for explaining Chain_SimulationStages-like systems.

entries.name: InitializeNeighborGrid
entries.summary: Initializes a Neighbor Grid data interface for spatial queries among particles.

inputs.name: Grid
inputs.role: Neighbor grid data interface to initialize.

inputs.name: World Bounds
inputs.role: Spatial region covered by the grid.

outputs.name: initialized grid
outputs.role: Cleared/prepared neighbor grid for population and lookup.

side_effects: Prepares GPU spatial acceleration for neighbor search.

critical_inputs.name: Grid
critical_inputs.why: Must be the same NeighborGrid data interface used by populate/query stages.

critical_inputs.name: World Bounds
critical_inputs.why: Particles outside bounds will not be found.

critical_inputs.name: Cell Size
critical_inputs.why: Controls precision and capacity tradeoff.

stack_requirements: Initialize before PopulateNeighborGrid and before any lookup/query stage.
required_followups: Verify all neighbor-grid modules reference the same grid and compatible bounds.
pitfalls: Must be paired with PopulateNeighborGrid and lookup logic; wrong bounds/cell size can make queries empty.
entries.notes: Relevant for flocking, constraints, collision, and particle-neighbor logic.

entries.name: PopulateNeighborGrid
entries.summary: Writes particle locations/indices into a Neighbor Grid for later neighbor lookup.

inputs.name: Grid
inputs.role: Neighbor grid data interface to populate.

inputs.name: Particle ID/Index
inputs.role: Particle identifier written into grid cells.

outputs.name: populated grid
outputs.role: Grid cells containing particle indices for lookup.

side_effects: Enables later modules/stages to query nearby particles.

critical_inputs.name: Grid
critical_inputs.why: Must match the initialized/query grid.

critical_inputs.name: Position
critical_inputs.why: Determines where each particle is binned.

critical_inputs.name: Particle ID/Index
critical_inputs.why: Determines what later lookup returns.

stack_requirements: Run after InitializeNeighborGrid and before lookup/constraint/query stages.
required_followups: Verify initialize -> populate -> query order and same grid data interface.
pitfalls: Order matters: initialize grid before populating, and query after population.
entries.notes: Core building block for neighbor-based GPU effects.
